sector.name: Writing Instruments
sector.description: Stationery and consumerware brands expanding categories

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹543 Cr, broadly stable YoY
Operating EBITDA stood at ₹59 Cr, with operating EBITDA margin of 11%
PAT stood at ₹33 Cr, with PAT margin of 6%
Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹138 Cr, down 11% YoY
Q4FY26 operating EBITDA stood at ₹18 Cr, with 13% margin; Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹10 Cr
Linc Ltd.outlook: Q4FY26 was weak due to moderation in corporate / institutional sales and export pressure. Management expects Q1FY27 to reflect similar trends, but triggers are visible from distribution restructuring, premium product focus, West Bengal facility, exports recovery and subsidiary scale-up.
highlights: Corporate gifting orders are large and lumpy, so they do not follow a predictable annual pattern
Export revenue was impacted by geopolitical issues in certain international markets
Mitsubishi Pencil JV operations remain stable and recently launched products received encouraging response
Morris subsidiary traction is linked to the upcoming West Bengal facility, expected to become operational by Q3FY27
Distribution strategy has been changed by splitting the sales team into mass distribution and premium brand verticals
Company added around 125 frontline salespeople on a base of around 350
Linc Ltd.quote: "Linc is not outperforming the sector currently on growth, but triggers are visible from distribution restructuring, premium product focus, West Bengal plant, exports recovery and subsidiary scale-up."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹2,326 Cr (+22% YoY), ahead of guidance
EBITDA stood at ₹403 Cr (+16% YoY), with EBITDA margin of 17%
PAT stood at ₹240 Cr (+12% YoY), with PAT margin of 10%
Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹604 Cr (+19% YoY); Q4FY26 EBITDA stood at ₹101 Cr (+14% YoY)
Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹58 Cr (+14% YoY), with PAT margin of 10%
highlights: Growth was driven by new product launches, domestic demand, exports and capacity additions
New launches included pencil boxes, school bags, pens, mechanical pencils, stamp pads, scholastic stationery, art, hobby and craft, kits and paper stationery
Q4FY26 growth was led by office supplies, hobby and craft and back-to-school categories
Core stationery grew around 19% in Q4FY26, driven by underlying back-to-school demand
Raw material cost increased around 15-17%, while pricing actions so far are around 4-5%
DOMS sees around 3-3.5 lakh stationery outlets in India, with around 2.25 lakh directly serviceable
DOMS Industries Ltd.quote: "DOMS is outperforming the sector on revenue growth, product innovation, brand strength, distribution reach, domestic demand and capacity-led growth visibility."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹1,250 Cr (+16% YoY), achieving the 15% growth guidance
Gross profit stood at ₹638 Cr (+16% YoY), with gross margin of 51%
EBITDA stood at ₹225 Cr (+22% YoY), with EBITDA margin of 18%
PAT stood at ₹141 Cr (+19% YoY), with PAT margin of 11%
Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹323 Cr (+8% YoY); Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹37 Cr (+18% YoY)
highlights: FY26 was a defining year as the company moved from being largely pen-centric to a more diversified business
Creative and Steel Bottles / Houseware now contribute 31% of overall revenue
Own brand sales contributed 91% of FY26 revenue vs 87% in FY25 and 80% in FY23
Domestic OEM business has been fully phased out with legacy OEM relationships reduced to zero
Creative segment revenue stood at ₹298 Cr in FY26, up 74%; Q4FY26 Creative revenue grew 80%
Steel Bottles and Houseware revenue stood at ₹85 Cr in FY26, up 95%
Flair Writing Industries Ltd.quote: "Flair is outperforming the sector on PAT growth, margin expansion, own brand mix, Creative growth and Steel Bottles / Houseware scale-up."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹2,324 Cr (+9% YoY)
EBITDA stood at ₹526 Cr, with EBITDA margin of 23%
PAT stood at ₹332 Cr, with PAT margin of 14%
Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹654 Cr (+11% YoY); Q4FY26 EBITDA stood at ₹137 Cr with 21% margin
Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹90 Cr, with PAT margin of 14%; cash flow from operations stood at ₹255 Cr
highlights: FY26 was a year of temporary consolidation due to multiple headwinds and softer demand in H2FY26
Growth was led by writing instruments, Opalware, glassware and selected consumerware products
Q4FY26 writing instruments revenue stood at ₹128 Cr, up 64%
E-commerce and quick-commerce together now contribute nearly 17% of overall revenue
Two additional steel bottle manufacturing lines were operationalized in Q4FY26 and four more were commissioned in Q1FY27
Glassware utilisation remains around 60%, impacted by dumping of imported glass products from China
Cello World Ltd.quote: "Cello is not outperforming the sector on FY26 growth, but it can improve if steel bottle ramp-up, writing instruments scale-up, glassware profitability and digital channel growth play out."
